qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Tiny PNG dimension bomb in addImage causes typed-array allocation failure
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The public entrypoint overlaps (`addImage`), but the root cause does not: this finding is the PNG branch (`processPNG` in `src/modules/png_support.js` reaching `fast-png`) while the advisory fixes oversized BMP allocation in `BmpDecoder.parseBGR` under `src/libs/BMPDecoder.js`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. GHSA-95fx-jjr5-f39c is the BMPDecoder dimension-allocation bug fixed in `src/libs/BMPDecoder.js`, while the session's only final qualifying finding is a separate PNG decoder allocation failure.
evidence: The advisory summary and fix commit `ae4b93f76d8fc1baa5614bd5fdb5d174c3b85f0d` describe unvalidated BMP dimensions in `BmpDecoder`, with the patch adding a size cap before `new Uint8Array(width * height * 4)` in `src/libs/BMPDecoder.js`.

The only manifested `qualifying_vuln` finding, `png_dimension_bomb_addimage`, instead traces `addImage` through `processPNG` and `decodePng(imageData, { checkCrc: true })` in `src/modules/png_support.js`, with proof centered on PNG IHDR dimensions and `fast-png` allocation failure.

The advisory's public summary explicitly names harmful BMP files and the patch only modifies BMP parsing, so the shared `addImage` entrypoint is not enough to treat the PNG issue as the same root cause.
